abstract: A manager that provides network-slicing capabilities for controlling and monitoring cellular network traffic routing.
overview:
## Overview

Use `CTSlicingManager` to control how your app’s network traffic routes through cellular network slices. Network slicing allows carriers to partition their network resources to provide different levels of service quality for specific types of apps. For example, a gaming slice might prioritize low latency, and a streaming slice might focus on consistent bandwidth. Your app can activate a preferred slice to ensure its traffic receives the appropriate quality of service.

Query [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSlicingManager/availableSliceAppCategories] to discover which slice categories your app can use.

Activate your preferred slice before creating network connections. New connections automatically route through the activated slice. Call [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSlicingManager/activatePreferredSliceForCategory(_:)] before establishing network connections.

Query [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSlicingManager/activeSlices] to verify activation and monitor network slice usage.

Call [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSlicingManager/disableSlicing()] to return to the default cellular connection when you no longer need slice-specific routing.
